match: Ric Flair vs. Butch Reed [ a ]
description: As NWA World Heavyweight Champion, Ric Flair frequently defended his coveted title across various territories. This encounter saw Flair pitted against the formidable Butch Reed, a powerful and athletic challenger looking to make his mark and potentially dethrone the "Nature Boy" in a high-profile CWF main event.

This match is a cornerstone of the legendary rivalry between Dynamite Kid and Tiger Mask, a series that redefined junior heavyweight wrestling. Their innovative, high-octane encounters pushed the boundaries of athleticism and technical skill, captivating audiences worldwide and setting a new standard for the division.

Representing the pinnacle of UWF's shoot-style wrestling, this bout pitted two of its most skilled practitioners, Kazuo Yamazaki and Nobuhiko Takada, against each other. Their clash was a pure display of realistic striking, grappling, and submission artistry, embodying the promotion's unique and intense philosophy.

match: Bruiser Brody and Stan Hansen vs. Dory Funk Jr. and Terry Funk
description: In the brutal Real World Tag League, the monstrous duo of Bruiser Brody and Stan Hansen collided with the legendary Dory Funk Jr. and Terry Funk. This high-stakes tournament match was a clash of styles and generations, pitting the violent, brawling foreign monsters against the revered, tough-as-nails Funk brothers in a contest for supremacy.

Kuniaki Kobayashi, a notorious rival of the original Tiger Mask, reignited his personal crusade against the masked legend when Mitsuharu Misawa donned the persona in AJPW. This match was a continuation of Kobayashi's relentless pursuit to expose and defeat Tiger Mask, often resorting to attempts to rip off his mask, adding intense personal stakes to their encounters.

This highly anticipated singles match pitted the dominant, legendary champion Jaguar Yokota against Lioness Asuka, one half of the incredibly popular Crush Gals. It was a generational clash of AJW titans, with Asuka aiming to prove her singles prowess against one of the sport's all-time greats on a major stage.

match: Jumbo Tsuruta and Genichiro Tenryu vs. Riki Choshu and Yoshiaki Yatsu
event: New Years War Super Battle Night 25
description: This monumental tag team clash pitted AJPW's top duo, Jumbo Tsuruta and Genichiro Tenryu, against the "Revolutionary Warriors," Riki Choshu and Yoshiaki Yatsu, who had defected from NJPW. It represented a heated inter-promotional war, with both teams vying for supremacy and the future of All Japan Pro Wrestling hanging in the balance.

At the prestigious Battle of the Belts 2, NWA World Heavyweight Champion Ric Flair defended his title against the incredibly talented and popular Barry Windham. Windham, a rising star, consistently pushed Flair to his limits, making every encounter a potential changing of the guard and a testament to their incredible in-ring chemistry.

match: The Fantastics ( Bobby Fulton and Tommy Rogers ) vs. The Sheepherders ( Butch Miller and Luke Williams )
description: In the prestigious Crockett Cup tag team tournament, the high-flying, athletic Fantastics battled the notoriously brawling and unpredictable Sheepherders. This clash of styles promised an exciting and violent encounter, as both teams fought to advance in one of professional wrestling's most significant tag team competitions.

The perennial rivalry between NWA World Champion Ric Flair and the popular Barry Windham continued on World Wide Wrestling. Windham, a fan favorite and legitimate threat, relentlessly pursued Flair's championship, making every encounter a testament to his determination to reach the pinnacle of professional wrestling.

This was a monumental clash between the two halves of the legendary Crush Gals, Chigusa Nagayo and Lioness Asuka. While partners, their intense singles rivalry was equally iconic, pitting the two biggest stars in AJW against each other for ultimate bragging rights and often, championship gold.

match: Akira Maeda and Nobuhiko Takada vs. Keiji Muto and Shiro Koshinaka
description: This tag team match epitomized the growing tension within NJPW between the shoot-style practitioners and the more traditional wrestling stars. Akira Maeda and Nobuhiko Takada, proponents of the realistic UWF style, faced off against the charismatic Keiji Muto and Shiro Koshinaka, representing the established New Japan philosophy, in a clash of ideologies.

At the prestigious Crockett Cup, NWA World Heavyweight Champion Ric Flair once again faced his perennial challenger, Barry Windham. This high-stakes encounter was another chapter in their legendary rivalry, with Windham desperately seeking to overcome the "Nature Boy" and claim the world title on one of JCP's biggest stages.

match: Paul Ellering , The Road Warriors ( Hawk and Animal ) , and The Super Powers ( Dusty Rhodes and Nikita Koloff ) vs. The Four Horsemen ( Arn Anderson , Lex Luger , Ric Flair , Tully Blanchard , and J. J. Dillon )
description: This was a monumental WarGames match at The Great American Bash, pitting the formidable Four Horsemen against a dream team of top babyfaces. The Road Warriors and Super Powers united to battle the Horsemen in the brutal double-cage structure, aiming to finally dismantle the dominant heel faction in one of wrestling's most iconic and violent stipulations.

match: Paul Ellering , The Road Warriors ( Hawk and Animal ) , and The Super Powers ( Dusty Rhodes and Nikita Koloff ) vs. The Four Horsemen ( Arn Anderson , Lex Luger , Ric Flair , The War Machine , and Tully Blanchard )
description: Another epic WarGames battle at The Great American Bash saw the powerful alliance of The Road Warriors and The Super Powers clash with the nefarious Four Horsemen. This brutal, multi-man, double-cage match was the ultimate showdown in their bitter rivalry, with both factions fighting for dominance and control in JCP.

match: Chigusa Nagayo , Mika Suzuki, Mika Takahashi, Yachiya Hirata, Yumi Ogura, and Yumiko Hotta vs. Etsuko Mita , Kazue Nagahori , Lioness Asuka , Mika Komatsu, Mitsuko Nishiwaki, and Sachiko Nakamura
description: This colossal 12-woman tag team match at Korakuen Hall showcased a massive faction war within AJW, with legendary rivals Chigusa Nagayo and Lioness Asuka captaining opposing teams. It was a spectacular display of talent, featuring a mix of established stars and rising contenders battling for supremacy in a chaotic and exciting encounter.

match: Genichiro Tenryu and Toshiaki Kawada vs. Stan Hansen and Terry Gordy
description: In the intense Real World Tag League, the powerful duo of Genichiro Tenryu and Toshiaki Kawada faced the monstrous foreign team of Stan Hansen and Terry Gordy. This high-stakes tournament bout was a brutal clash of styles, with both teams vying for critical points and a chance to win All Japan's most prestigious tag team competition.

match: Genichiro Tenryu , Toshiaki Kawada , and Samson Fuyuki vs. Jumbo Tsuruta , Yoshiaki Yatsu , and Masanobu Fuchi
description: This explosive six-man tag match was a direct confrontation between the factions of Jumbo Tsuruta and Genichiro Tenryu, following Tenryu's defection to form his Revolutionary Army. It represented a pivotal moment in AJPW, as the former partners and their allies battled for control and supremacy within the promotion.

The ultimate clash of opposites that redefined professional wrestling. On one side stands Ric Flair: the stylin', profilin', jet-flyin' 'Nature Boy,' an arrogant multi-time world champion who represents the excesses and villainy of the 1980s. On the other side is Ricky 'The Dragon' Steamboat, the quintessential virtuous family man, universally beloved for his honor and peerless athletic ability. Their deeply personal rivalry comes to a head here at the Chi-Town Rumble, as Steamboat seeks to finally capture the NWA World Heavyweight Championship from his ultimate nemesis in what is widely considered one of the greatest technical displays of all time.

The second legendary chapter of the defining rivalry of 1989. Following Ricky Steamboat's monumental victory at the Chi-Town Rumble to capture the NWA World Heavyweight Championship, an irate Ric Flair immediately demanded a rematch. This time, they meet at Clash of the Champions VI under a grueling Two out of Three Falls stipulation. The history between these two masters guarantees a deeply strategic and fiercely competitive encounter. With the 'Nature Boy' desperate to reclaim his throne and the 'Dragon' determined to prove his title reign is no fluke, the stage is set for a true marathon of wrestling excellence.

The epic, final conclusion to the greatest trilogy in wrestling history. After their time-limit classic at Clash of the Champions, the controversy surrounding the NWA World Heavyweight Championship demands a decisive winner. Ric Flair and Ricky Steamboat clash at WrestleWar with a special set of rules: judges are at ringside to ensure a winner is crowned if the time limit expires. The deep respect mixed with intense competitive fire between these two legends has reached a boiling point. Both men know each other's every counter and move, making this final encounter the ultimate test of their legendary careers.

Even after their legendary trilogy, the rivalry between NWA World Champion Ric Flair and Ricky Steamboat continued to captivate audiences. This encounter at WrestleWar showcased their enduring chemistry, as Steamboat once again challenged the "Nature Boy" in a testament to their unparalleled in-ring battles and mutual respect.

This highly anticipated singles match was a pivotal moment in the bitter rivalry between former tag team partners, Jumbo Tsuruta and Genichiro Tenryu. Following Tenryu's defection to form his Revolutionary Army, this bout represented a direct clash for supremacy, with both men fighting to establish themselves as the undisputed ace of AJPW.

This brutal "I Quit" match was the violent culmination of a deeply personal feud between NWA World Heavyweight Champion Ric Flair and the deranged Terry Funk. Sparked by Funk's vicious piledriver on a table, this encounter saw both men push themselves to their absolute limits, refusing to yield until one was forced to utter those two fateful words.

match: Jushin Thunder Liger vs. Naoki Sano
description: This match featured two of NJPW's most dynamic junior heavyweights, Jushin Thunder Liger and Naoki Sano, in a high-octane encounter. Their rivalry was known for its incredible athleticism and innovative offense, consistently delivering thrilling contests that elevated the junior heavyweight division.

A historic passing of the torch that altered the landscape of Japanese professional wrestling forever. Jumbo Tsuruta, the undisputed ace and virtually unbeatable legend of All Japan Pro Wrestling, faces off against the rising star Mitsuharu Misawa. Just weeks prior, Misawa dramatically unmasked himself, abandoning the Tiger Mask II persona to compete under his true identity. This match is a profound collision of generations: the dominant, established veteran against the hungry, incredibly gifted challenger looking to lead the company into a new era. The crowd in the Budokan is absolutely electric, sensing a monumental shift in history.

match: Jumbo Tsuruta and Akira Taue vs. Mitsuharu Misawa and Toshiaki Kawada
description: This pivotal tag team match intensified the generational war in AJPW, pitting the established ace Jumbo Tsuruta and his ally Akira Taue against the rising stars Mitsuharu Misawa and Toshiaki Kawada. It was a crucial battle in the ongoing struggle for supremacy between Tsuruta's veteran faction and the burgeoning new guard.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa , Toshiaki Kawada , and Kenta Kobashi vs. Jumbo Tsuruta , Masanobu Fuchi , and Akira Taue
description: This epic six-man tag team encounter was a quintessential clash between Jumbo Tsuruta's veteran faction and the formidable "Super Generation Army" of Mitsuharu Misawa, Toshiaki Kawada, and Kenta Kobashi. It was a defining battle in the generational war, showcasing the relentless challenge of the young lions against the established order of AJPW.

This hard-hitting singles match at Korakuen Hall pitted two of AJW's most iconic and powerful wrestlers, Akira Hokuto and Bull Nakano, against each other. Known for their intense brawling and incredible athleticism, their encounters were always high-stakes affairs, showcasing the brutal beauty of joshi puroresu.

match: The Four Horsemen ( Barry Windham , Larry Zbyszko , Ric Flair , and Sid Vicious ) vs. Brian Pillman , Sting , and The Steiner Brothers ( Rick Steiner and Scott Steiner )
description: At WrestleWar 1991, the legendary Four Horsemen clashed with a formidable alliance of WCW's top babyfaces in a brutal WarGames match. This iconic double-cage battle saw Sting, Brian Pillman, and The Steiner Brothers attempt to overcome the Horsemen's dominance, culminating in one of the most violent and memorable WarGames encounters ever.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa , Toshiaki Kawada , and Kenta Kobashi vs. Jumbo Tsuruta , Masanobu Fuchi , and Akira Taue
description: This six-man tag team match, held on Fan Appreciation Day, continued the intense generational conflict in AJPW between Jumbo Tsuruta's veteran faction and the "Super Generation Army" of Misawa, Kawada, and Kobashi. These recurring clashes were vital in establishing the new guard's legitimacy while Jumbo fought to maintain his dominance.

match: Big Van Vader vs. Keiji Mutoh
description: In the prestigious G1 Climax tournament, the unstoppable force of Big Van Vader collided with the charismatic and athletic Keiji Mutoh. This high-stakes tournament match was a classic power-vs-skill encounter, as Mutoh attempted to overcome Vader's overwhelming strength and brutality to advance in New Japan's premier competition.

This highly anticipated singles match at Wrestlemarinepiad showcased two of AJW's most athletic and innovative stars, Kyoko Inoue and Manami Toyota. Their encounters were renowned for breathtaking high-flying maneuvers and intense technical exchanges, representing the pinnacle of 90s joshi puroresu.

match: El Samurai vs. Jushin Thunder Liger
description: In a high-stakes encounter on the Explosion Tour, the legendary Jushin Thunder Liger defended his status as NJPW's top junior heavyweight against the formidable El Samurai. This match was a showcase of innovative offense and incredible athleticism, a testament to the depth and excitement of New Japan's junior heavyweight division.

match: Atsushi Onita , El Hijo del Santo and Tarzan Goto vs. Horace Boulder , Negro Casas and Tim Patterson
description: The birthplace of an era-defining rivalry. Bret Hart enters as the traditional, beloved hero who feels the fans and the company have turned their backs on him. On the other side is Stone Cold Steve Austin, the foul-mouthed, anti-establishment rebel who has ruthlessly tormented Hart for months. With UFC legend Ken Shamrock as the special guest referee, they face off in a Submission Match where the only way to win is to make the other man quit. It is a masterclass in psychology waiting to unfold.

match: Dangerous Alliance ( Arn Anderson , Bobby Eaton , Larry Zbyszko , Rick Rude , and Steve Austin ) vs. Sting's Squadron ( Barry Windham , Dustin Rhodes , Nikita Koloff , Ricky Steamboat , and Sting )
description: One of the greatest, most emotionally charged WarGames matches ever conceived. The brilliantly sinister Paul E. Dangerously has assembled the 'Dangerous Alliance,' a stable of some of the most lethal and talented wrestlers of the era, including Rick Rude, Larry Zbyszko, Bobby Eaton, Arn Anderson, and a fiery young Steve Austin. Their goal is the complete destruction of WCW. Standing in their way is 'Sting's Squadron,' a team built around the franchise icon Sting, alongside Ricky Steamboat, Dustin Rhodes, Nikita Koloff, and Barry Windham. The history and sheer hatred between these ten men guarantee absolute carnage inside the double cage.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa , Toshiaki Kawada , and Kenta Kobashi vs. Jumbo Tsuruta , Masanobu Fuchi , and Akira Taue
description: This intense six-man tag team match continued the epic generational struggle in AJPW, as the "Super Generation Army" of Misawa, Kawada, and Kobashi once again confronted Jumbo Tsuruta's veteran faction. These recurring battles were crucial in defining the future of the promotion, showcasing the relentless drive of the younger stars against the established order.

match: Kenta Kobashi and Tsuyoshi Kikuchi vs. Can-Am Express ( Doug Furnas and Dan Kroffat )
description: This highly acclaimed tag team match showcased the incredible talent of AJPW's junior heavyweight division, pitting the hard-hitting Kenta Kobashi and Tsuyoshi Kikuchi against the athletic and technical Can-Am Express. It was a fast-paced, high-impact encounter that exemplified the exciting tag team action of the era.

match: Kenta Kobashi and Tsuyoshi Kikuchi vs. Masanobu Fuchi and Yoshinari Ogawa
description: This tag team contest featured the popular duo of Kenta Kobashi and Tsuyoshi Kikuchi battling the veteran Masanobu Fuchi and the skilled Yoshinari Ogawa. It was a solid display of All Japan's tag team prowess, with the younger team aiming to overcome the experience and cunning of their opponents.

This high-impact singles match at Mid Summer Typhoon featured two of AJW's most dynamic and innovative stars, Manami Toyota and Toshiyo Yamada. Their encounters were renowned for their breathtaking athleticism, rapid-fire exchanges, and incredible high-flying, consistently delivering some of the most exciting joshi puroresu of the era.

match: Kyoko Inoue and Takako Inoue vs. Cutie Suzuki and Mayumi Ozaki
description: Dream Slam I marked a monumental inter-promotional event between AJW and JWP, bringing together top talent from both companies. This tag team encounter pitted AJW's formidable Inoue duo against JWP's dynamic Cutie Suzuki and Mayumi Ozaki, symbolizing the fierce rivalry and the desire for supremacy between the promotions. It was a showcase of distinct wrestling styles and a battle for bragging rights.

As the main event of the historic Dream Slam I, this match was a colossal inter-promotional showdown between two of joshi puroresu's most revered and toughest competitors. Shinobu Kandori, representing LLPW, faced off against AJW's "Dangerous Queen" Akira Hokuto in a brutal, no-holds-barred contest. The bout was a definitive battle for promotional pride and individual supremacy, cementing its place as a legendary encounter.

match: Manami Toyota and Toshiyo Yamada vs. Dynamite Kansai and Mayumi Ozaki
description: Following the success of Dream Slam I, this tag team match at Dream Slam II continued the intense inter-promotional rivalry between AJW and JWP. AJW's high-flying aces, Manami Toyota and Toshiyo Yamada, clashed with JWP's hard-hitting, rebellious duo of Dynamite Kansai and Mayumi Ozaki. It was a high-stakes battle for tag team dominance and a further testament to the competitive spirit between the two premier joshi promotions.

This was a monumental clash between two titans of joshi puroresu, pitting the dominant, iconic heel Bull Nakano against the respected veteran and JWP legend, Devil Masami. It represented a generational and stylistic battle, with both women known for their incredible power and intensity. The match was a testament to their enduring legacies and a significant main event for JWP.

This was a pivotal encounter between two of All Japan Pro Wrestling's legendary "Four Pillars," Kenta Kobashi and Toshiaki Kawada, during the prestigious Champion Carnival tournament. Their matches were always intensely physical, deeply personal, and critical to the tournament standings, often determining who would advance to the finals. This bout epitomized their brutal rivalry and the fighting spirit of AJPW.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa , Kenta Kobashi , and Jun Akiyama vs. Yoshinari Ogawa and Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue )
description: This multi-man tag team match showcased the intense faction warfare that defined All Japan Pro Wrestling during its golden era. Mitsuharu Misawa, Kenta Kobashi, and Jun Akiyama, representing Misawa's formidable unit, clashed with the relentless Holy Demon Army of Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue, joined by Yoshinari Ogawa. These encounters were a cornerstone of AJPW's main event scene, highlighting the deep-seated rivalries and incredible chemistry between the "Four Pillars" and their allies.

This was a monumental test for the rising star Kenta Kobashi, as he faced the legendary "Lariat" Stan Hansen, one of All Japan's most feared gaijin. Hansen was known for his incredibly stiff and brutal style, making this match a true proving ground for Kobashi's toughness and main event potential. It was a classic clash of generations and styles, with Kobashi aiming to overcome a revered, dangerous veteran.

match: Aja Kong , Kyoko Inoue , Takako Inoue , and Sakie Hasegawa vs. Cutie Suzuki , Dynamite Kansai , Mayumi Ozaki , and Hikari Fukuoka
description: This epic 8-woman tag team match, part of the "Thunder Queen Battle" event, was a grand-scale continuation of the intense inter-promotional wars between AJW and JWP. It assembled a dream team of AJW powerhouses, including Aja Kong and the Inoue sisters, against JWP's fierce contingent led by Dynamite Kansai and Mayumi Ozaki. The match was a spectacular showcase of talent and a battle for ultimate bragging rights between the two premier joshi promotions.

Kenta Kobashi faced another formidable challenge in "Dr. Death" Steve Williams, a powerful gaijin known for his amateur wrestling prowess and incredibly stiff offense. This match was a crucial test for Kobashi, demonstrating his ability to overcome diverse and dangerous opponents on his path to becoming a main event pillar. It was a hard-hitting encounter that further solidified Kobashi's reputation for toughness and resilience.

match: The Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue ) vs. Kenta Kobashi and Mitsuharu Misawa
description: This was the epic final of All Japan Pro Wrestling's prestigious Real World Tag League, pitting the greatest tag team rivalry of the era against each other. Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi, the Super Generation Army, battled their arch-rivals, The Holy Demon Army (Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue), in a brutal, high-stakes encounter. The winner would claim the coveted tournament trophy and cement their dominance as the top tag team in the world.

match: Manami Toyota and Toshiyo Yamada vs. Dynamite Kansai and Mayumi Ozaki
description: This highly anticipated rematch saw AJW's dynamic duo, Manami Toyota and Toshiyo Yamada, once again clash with the hard-hitting team of Dynamite Kansai and Mayumi Ozaki. Building on their previous encounters, this match at the St. Battle Final likely carried significant championship or tournament implications. It further intensified the rivalry between these two elite tag teams, showcasing their incredible chemistry and competitive spirit.

match: Manami Toyota and Akira Hokuto vs. Toshiyo Yamada and Kyoko Inoue
description: This star-studded tag team match from AJW's "Tag League The Best" tournament brought together four of the promotion's most iconic talents. The high-flying Manami Toyota teamed with the "Dangerous Queen" Akira Hokuto to face the powerful duo of Toshiyo Yamada and Kyoko Inoue. It was a high-stakes tournament encounter, showcasing incredible athleticism and the deep talent pool of AJW's roster.

match: Manami Toyota and Akira Hokuto vs. Toshiyo Yamada and Kyoko Inoue
description: Another pivotal encounter within AJW's "Tag League The Best" tournament, this match once again saw the formidable pairing of Manami Toyota and Akira Hokuto take on Toshiyo Yamada and Kyoko Inoue. These four joshi legends delivered a high-octane performance, with each team vying for critical points to advance in the prestigious tag team competition. The bout was a testament to the incredible depth of talent and the competitive nature of AJW's tag division.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa , Kenta Kobashi , and Giant Baba vs. Masanobu Fuchi and Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue )
description: This special six-man tag match was a unique blend of generations and rivalries, featuring AJPW founder Giant Baba teaming with his top stars, Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi. They faced the formidable Holy Demon Army (Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue) alongside veteran Masanobu Fuchi. It was a ceremonial yet significant encounter, allowing Baba to share the ring with his main event talent and adding a unique historical flavor to the New Year Giant Series.

This legendary Ladder Match at WrestleMania X was fought to determine the undisputed Intercontinental Champion, as both Razor Ramon and Shawn Michaels laid claim to the title. Their groundbreaking performance not only stole the show but also revolutionized the ladder match concept, setting a new standard for high-stakes, innovative wrestling. It remains one of the most iconic matches in WWE history, a testament to their incredible chemistry and athleticism.

match: The Great Sasuke vs. Wild Pegasus
description: This was a pivotal match from the revolutionary Super J-Cup tournament, a groundbreaking event that gathered the world's best junior heavyweights. The Great Sasuke, representing Michinoku Pro, clashed with NJPW's Wild Pegasus (Chris Benoit) in a high-octane, innovative contest. Their incredible athleticism and crisp execution helped define the junior heavyweight style of the era, solidifying the tournament's legendary status.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi vs. The Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue )
description: This was another chapter in the legendary tag team rivalry between Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi, facing their perennial adversaries, The Holy Demon Army (Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue). As a staple of All Japan's main event scene, these matches consistently delivered brutal, high-impact action and further fueled the intense, personal animosity between the "Four Pillars." Each encounter was a battle for supremacy in AJPW's tag division.

This was a monumental singles clash between Mitsuharu Misawa and Toshiaki Kawada, the undisputed greatest rivalry in All Japan Pro Wrestling history. Their matches were intensely personal, brutally physical, and often carried the highest stakes, frequently for the Triple Crown Heavyweight Championship. This encounter epitomized their legendary feud, showcasing unparalleled intensity and a relentless desire for victory from both men.

match: The Great Sasuke vs. Jushin Thunder Liger
description: This was a dream match between two of the most influential and iconic junior heavyweights in professional wrestling history: The Great Sasuke and Jushin Thunder Liger. Sasuke, the innovative high-flyer and founder of Michinoku Pro, faced the established legend and NJPW ace, Liger. Their encounter was a masterclass of athleticism and technical prowess, showcasing the pinnacle of the junior heavyweight style.

This iconic Steel Cage match at SummerSlam was the culmination of one of the most compelling brother vs. brother feuds in professional wrestling history. Owen Hart, having betrayed his older brother Bret, challenged "The Hitman" for the WWF Championship in a deeply personal and intensely physical encounter. The match captivated audiences, showcasing their incredible family rivalry and technical wrestling prowess.

match: Kyoko Inoue and Takako Inoue vs. Manami Toyota and Toshiyo Yamada
description: This highly competitive tag team match pitted the formidable Inoue sisters, Kyoko and Takako, against arguably the greatest joshi tag team of all time, Manami Toyota and Toshiyo Yamada. Taking place at the Wrestlemarinpiad event, it was a showcase of AJW's incredible tag team division and the deep talent within its roster. The bout delivered a high-octane performance, with both teams vying for supremacy and championship contention.

match: Los Gringos Locos ( Art Barr and Eddy Guerrero ) vs. El Hijo del Santo and Octagón
description: This iconic Hair vs. Mask match was the main event of AAA's groundbreaking "When Worlds Collide" pay-per-view, introducing Lucha Libre to a global audience. The hated heel duo of Los Gringos Locos (Art Barr and Eddy Guerrero) faced the beloved tecnicos, El Hijo del Santo and Octagón, in a high-stakes, emotionally charged encounter. The match is celebrated for its incredible action and its pivotal role in showcasing Mexican wrestling to the world.

This was a monumental singles match from the historic Big Egg Wrestling Universe show at the Tokyo Dome, a defining event for joshi puroresu. The dominant and fearsome Aja Kong clashed with the high-flying, acrobatic ace Manami Toyota in a battle of contrasting styles and immense star power. It was a main event caliber encounter on the grandest stage, showcasing the pinnacle of women's wrestling.

match: Rey Misterio Jr. vs. Juventud Guerrera
description: This early encounter between Rey Misterio Jr. and Juventud Guerrera showcased the incredible athleticism and innovative high-flying style that would soon define a generation of cruiserweight wrestling. Before their global fame, these two young luchadores delivered a breathtaking performance in AAA, laying the groundwork for their legendary rivalry. It was a glimpse into the future of fast-paced, aerial combat.

This was another intense singles encounter between two of All Japan Pro Wrestling's "Four Pillars," Kenta Kobashi and Toshiaki Kawada, during the New Year Giant Series. Their matches were renowned for their unparalleled physicality and deep-seated rivalry, often serving as pivotal moments in AJPW's championship landscape. This bout further solidified their status as two of the toughest and most compelling competitors in puroresu history.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi vs. The Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue )
description: This was a quintessential All Japan Pro Wrestling main event, pitting the beloved team of Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi against their arch-rivals, The Holy Demon Army (Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue). As a cornerstone of AJPW's golden era, these tag team battles were known for their incredible intensity, brutal exchanges, and the profound personal animosity between the "Four Pillars." It was a high-stakes clash for tag team supremacy.

match: Kenta Kobashi and Mitsuharu Misawa vs. Johnny Ace and Steve Williams
description: This tag team encounter saw All Japan's beloved aces, Kenta Kobashi and Mitsuharu Misawa, face the formidable gaijin duo of Johnny Ace and "Dr. Death" Steve Williams. These clashes between native heroes and powerful foreign invaders were a hallmark of AJPW's main event scene. The match showcased a compelling battle of styles and a test of strength against the dominant gaijin contingent.

This was the highly anticipated final of All Japan Pro Wrestling's prestigious Champion Carnival tournament, pitting the company's ace, Mitsuharu Misawa, against the often-underestimated Akira Taue. For Taue, a member of the Holy Demon Army and the "fourth" Pillar, this was a career-defining opportunity to defeat Misawa and win his first major singles tournament. The match represented a significant moment in Taue's journey to the top.

This was a premier singles encounter between two of All Japan Women's Pro-Wrestling's most celebrated and versatile stars, Manami Toyota and Kyoko Inoue. Toyota's unparalleled athleticism and high-flying prowess clashed with Inoue's powerful and technical style in a match that often carried championship implications. It was a showcase of their incredible rivalry and the depth of talent at the top of joshi puroresu.

match: Kenta Kobashi and Mitsuharu Misawa vs. The Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue )
description: This was yet another chapter in the legendary tag team saga between Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi and their bitter rivals, The Holy Demon Army (Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue). These main event clashes were the lifeblood of All Japan Pro Wrestling, consistently delivering brutal, emotionally charged action. Each encounter further solidified their status as the greatest tag team rivalry in wrestling history.

This was a quintessential clash of styles and a defining rivalry in All Japan Women's Pro-Wrestling, pitting the high-flying, acrobatic ace Manami Toyota against the dominant, fearsome monster heel Aja Kong. Their matches were often brutal, high-stakes affairs, frequently contested for the prestigious WWWA World Heavyweight Championship. This encounter showcased the incredible resilience of Toyota against Kong's overwhelming power.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa , Kenta Kobashi , and Satoru Asako vs. Tamon Honda and Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue )
description: This six-man tag team match continued the intense faction warfare that defined All Japan Pro Wrestling's main event scene. Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi, joined by Satoru Asako, faced off against the formidable Holy Demon Army (Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue) and veteran Tamon Honda. These encounters were crucial for showcasing the deep rivalries and incredible talent across AJPW's roster.

This singles match from AJW's prestigious Japan Grand Prix tournament saw the legendary Manami Toyota face Mima Shimoda, a formidable competitor often associated with the villainous Las Cachorras Orientales stable. As a tournament encounter, the stakes were high, with both women vying for crucial points to advance. It showcased Toyota's incredible skill against Shimoda's aggressive style in a competitive battle.

match: Kyoko Inoue and Takako Inoue vs. Manami Toyota and Sakie Hasegawa
description: This high-stakes tag team match from AJW's "WWWA Champions Night" pitted the powerful Inoue sisters, Kyoko and Takako, against the dynamic duo of Manami Toyota and Sakie Hasegawa. With the event's title suggesting championship implications, this bout was a showcase of AJW's elite tag team division. It delivered a competitive and thrilling encounter between four of the promotion's top stars.

This was a dream match and a true clash of titans in joshi puroresu, pitting the "Dangerous Queen" Akira Hokuto against the high-flying innovator Manami Toyota. Both women were at the absolute pinnacle of their careers, making this a legendary encounter that showcased their contrasting styles and unparalleled talent. It was a defining moment for AJW, delivering an unforgettable, high-impact singles contest.

This early encounter between Rey Misterio Jr. and Psicosis was a thrilling display of the innovative high-flying style that would soon captivate audiences worldwide. Their rivalry in AAA was legendary, marked by breathtaking athleticism and groundbreaking aerial maneuvers. This match was a precursor to their iconic battles and a testament to their profound impact on lucha libre and professional wrestling.

match: Juventud Guerrera vs. Rey Misterio Jr.
description: This match was a pivotal moment for ECW, introducing the electrifying, high-flying style of Lucha Libre to a wider American audience through two of its brightest young stars, Juventud Guerrera and Rey Misterio Jr. Their incredible athleticism and innovative maneuvers captivated fans, foreshadowing the cruiserweight revolution that would soon sweep through mainstream wrestling. It was a groundbreaking showcase of speed and agility.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa and Jun Akiyama vs. The Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue )
description: This tag team match saw AJPW's ace, Mitsuharu Misawa, team with the promising young star Jun Akiyama against their perennial rivals, The Holy Demon Army (Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue). These encounters were crucial for Akiyama's development, as he proved his mettle against the toughest competition. It continued the intense faction warfare and showcased the evolving dynamics within AJPW's main event scene.

match: Jun Akiyama and Mitsuharu Misawa vs. Johnny Ace and Steve Williams
description: This tag team encounter pitted the formidable duo of Mitsuharu Misawa and Jun Akiyama against the powerful gaijin team of Johnny Ace and "Dr. Death" Steve Williams. It was a classic All Japan dynamic, with the native heroes facing a stiff challenge from the dominant foreign contingent. The match served as another important test for Akiyama, showcasing his growth against top-tier international competition.

match: Kaientai DX ( Dick Togo , Men's Teioh , Shiryu , Shoichi Funaki , and Taka Michinoku ) vs. Gran Hamada , Gran Naniwa , Masato Yakushiji, Super Delfin , and Tiger Mask IV
description: This spectacular 10-man tag team match was a highlight of Michinoku Pro's 3rd Anniversary show, showcasing the promotion's unique high-flying and innovative style. The villainous Kaientai DX stable, led by Taka Michinoku, clashed with a dream team of Michinoku Pro's top babyfaces and legends, including Gran Hamada and Super Delfin. It was a chaotic, fast-paced, and memorable main event that epitomized the promotion's exciting junior heavyweight action.

match: Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue ) vs. Jun Akiyama and Mitsuharu Misawa
description: The Real World Tag League final pitted the dominant Holy Demon Army against the formidable duo of Mitsuharu Misawa and Jun Akiyama. This match was a battle for supremacy in All Japan's tag division, showcasing the established champions against a rising, powerful team. The stakes were high, with the winners cementing their place as the promotion's top tag team.

This was a pivotal early chapter in one of professional wrestling's most iconic rivalries, pitting the stoic ace Mitsuharu Misawa against the fiery Kenta Kobashi. As two of All Japan's burgeoning Four Pillars, each sought to establish dominance and prove themselves as the future of the promotion. The match was a testament to their burgeoning chemistry and the intense competition for the Triple Crown Heavyweight Championship picture.

match: Bret Hart vs. Stone Cold Steve Austin
description: This legendary "Submission Match" marked a pivotal double-turn, transforming Stone Cold Steve Austin into the WWF's ultimate anti-hero and Bret Hart into a bitter, disgruntled villain. The brutal, blood-soaked encounter cemented Austin's status as a main event player and redefined the landscape of the Attitude Era. It's widely regarded as one of the greatest WrestleMania matches of all time.

event: Best of the Super Juniors IV Night 17 (Final)
description: The Best of the Super Juniors IV Final saw two of New Japan's premier junior heavyweights, Koji Kanemoto and El Samurai, clash for tournament supremacy. This high-stakes encounter determined who would be crowned the year's top junior heavyweight, a prestigious accolade in NJPW. The victor would gain significant momentum and a potential shot at the IWGP Junior Heavyweight Championship.

This was another brutal chapter in the deeply personal and violent rivalry between Mitsuharu Misawa and Toshiaki Kawada, two of All Japan's revered Four Pillars. Their matches were known for their incredible intensity and stiff strikes, as both men sought to prove their superiority and claim the top spot in the promotion. The stakes were always high, often involving the Triple Crown Heavyweight Championship, and their encounters were legendary for their sheer physicality.

The culmination of a legendary rivalry that spanned decades. Following their incredible first encounter, Shawn Michaels became obsessed with ending The Undertaker's undefeated WrestleMania streak. When he failed to secure a rematch by normal means, he cost Taker the World Heavyweight Championship, forcing the Deadman's hand. Taker agreed to the rematch under one condition: if Michaels lost, his career would be over. The stakes have never been higher—the greatest streak in wrestling history vs. the career of the greatest in-ring performer of all time.

match: The Holy Demon Army ( Toshiaki Kawada and Akira Taue ) vs. Jun Akiyama and Mitsuharu Misawa
description: The Real World Tag League final pitted the dominant Holy Demon Army against the formidable duo of Mitsuharu Misawa and Jun Akiyama in a clash for tag team supremacy. This highly anticipated match represented a generational struggle, with the established champions defending their legacy against a powerful, ascendant team. The winners would solidify their status as the premier tag team in All Japan Pro Wrestling.

This RINGS encounter showcased two of the promotion's most legitimate shoot-style fighters, Kiyoshi Tamura and Tsuyoshi Kosaka, in a high-stakes contest. RINGS emphasized realistic combat, and this match was a pure display of their grappling and striking prowess. The bout was a significant test for both men, determining who held the upper hand in the competitive RINGS landscape.

On All Japan's 26th Anniversary Show, the legendary rivalry between Kenta Kobashi and Mitsuharu Misawa reached another epic chapter. These two titans, cornerstones of the promotion, consistently delivered brutal and emotional encounters that defined an era. This match was a testament to their enduring chemistry and their relentless pursuit of supremacy, often with the Triple Crown Heavyweight Championship on the line.

This was a pivotal early chapter in one of professional wrestling's most iconic rivalries, pitting the stoic ace Mitsuharu Misawa against the fiery Kenta Kobashi. As two of All Japan's burgeoning Four Pillars, each sought to establish dominance and prove themselves as the future of the promotion. The match was a testament to their burgeoning chemistry and the intense competition for the Triple Crown Heavyweight Championship picture.

match: Mitsuharu Misawa and Yoshinari Ogawa vs. Burning ( Kenta Kobashi and Jun Akiyama )
description: This star-studded tag team encounter pitted the legendary Mitsuharu Misawa and Yoshinari Ogawa against the formidable Burning duo of Kenta Kobashi and Jun Akiyama. The match showcased the intense factional warfare and intertwined rivalries that defined All Japan Pro Wrestling during its golden era. With multiple top-tier talents involved, it was a high-stakes battle for bragging rights and supremacy within the promotion.

match: Masanobu Fuchi and Toshiaki Kawada vs. Takashi Iizuka and Yuji Nagata
description: This rare inter-promotional tag team match saw All Japan's Toshiaki Kawada and Masanobu Fuchi invade New Japan to face Takashi Iizuka and Yuji Nagata. It was a significant clash of styles and company pride, as two of AJPW's toughest veterans took on NJPW's formidable young lions. The bout represented a unique opportunity for both promotions to prove their superiority.

This highly anticipated encounter brought the legendary rivalry between Mitsuharu Misawa and Kenta Kobashi into Pro Wrestling Noah, the promotion Misawa founded. Having battled countless times in All Japan, their feud continued with renewed intensity, often for the prestigious GHC Heavyweight Championship. This match represented a pinnacle of their careers in Noah, showcasing their enduring chemistry and the emotional weight of their storied rivalry.

This pivotal match in Pro Wrestling Noah saw Kenta Kobashi clash with Jun Akiyama, two former tag partners from their "Burning" days in All Japan who had become fierce rivals. Both men were cornerstones of the newly formed Noah, and this encounter was crucial in establishing the pecking order within the promotion. The "Departure" event itself marked a new era, and this match was a defining moment for both competitors.

This highly anticipated rematch, "Joe vs. Punk II," followed their groundbreaking 60-minute draw, raising the stakes and expectations for their Ring of Honor rivalry. Both Samoa Joe and CM Punk were at the peak of their independent wrestling careers, vying for supremacy and the ROH World Championship. The match was a testament to their incredible chemistry and their ability to deliver a compelling, athletic contest that captivated the wrestling world.

match: Christopher Daniels vs. AJ Styles vs. Samoa Joe
description: This legendary three-way match for the TNA X-Division Championship is widely regarded as one of the greatest matches in TNA history, showcasing the unparalleled talent of Christopher Daniels, AJ Styles, and Samoa Joe. Each competitor was at the top of their game, pushing the boundaries of athletic, high-flying, and hard-hitting wrestling. The bout solidified the X-Division's reputation for innovative, must-see action and cemented the legacies of all three men.

This dream match brought together two of the most respected heavyweights from different eras and continents: Ring of Honor's dominant Samoa Joe and All Japan/Noah legend Kenta Kobashi. Kobashi's rare appearance in an American independent promotion made this a truly historic and unforgettable encounter. The clash of styles and the sheer intensity of both men created an instant classic, elevating ROH's profile and delivering a once-in-a-lifetime spectacle.

match: Cima , Masato Yoshino , and Naruki Doi vs. Dragon Kid , Genki Horiguchi , and Ryo Saito
description: This exhilarating six-man tag team match showcased the unique, high-octane style of Japan's Dragon Gate promotion to a captivated Ring of Honor audience. Featuring top Dragon Gate stars like Cima, Masato Yoshino, and Dragon Kid, the bout was a dazzling display of intricate teamwork, lightning-fast maneuvers, and breathtaking athleticism. It was a landmark cross-promotional encounter that introduced a new level of junior heavyweight action to the American independent scene.

Set in Punk's hometown of Chicago, this match is fueled by the iconic 'Summer of Punk' storyline. With his contract set to expire at midnight following this very event, CM Punk threatened to defeat the franchise player John Cena and leave WWE with the WWE Championship in tow. A desperate Vince McMahon promised severe consequences for Cena if he let that happen. The atmosphere is electric as an intensely partisan, anti-Cena crowd prepares to witness whether Punk will actually walk out of the company as the supreme champion.

This intense Ring of Honor World Championship match pitted the defending champion, Davey Richards, against the powerful and determined challenger, Michael Elgin. Both men were known for their hard-hitting, high-impact styles, promising a grueling contest for the promotion's top prize. The bout was a testament to ROH's reputation for delivering athletic and competitive main events.

This IWGP Heavyweight Championship clash saw New Japan's beloved "Ace," Hiroshi Tanahashi, face the terrifying and legitimate threat of Minoru Suzuki. Suzuki's sadistic submission style and no-nonsense attitude provided a stark contrast to Tanahashi's heroic resilience. The match was a brutal test for the champion, highlighting the contrasting philosophies and intense competition at the top of NJPW.

This was another pivotal chapter in the defining rivalry between Hiroshi Tanahashi and Kazuchika Okada, as the "Ace" defended the IWGP Heavyweight Championship against the "Rainmaker." Okada, having already proven he could defeat Tanahashi, sought to firmly establish his era, while Tanahashi fought to maintain his reign. Their matches were legendary for their drama and high stakes, shaping the future of New Japan Pro Wrestling.

This G1 Climax encounter was a brutal, hard-hitting spectacle between two of New Japan's toughest competitors, Katsuyori Shibata and Tomohiro Ishii. Known for their incredibly stiff strikes and unwavering resolve, both men delivered a match that was pure, unfiltered violence and fighting spirit. It quickly became a fan-favorite, showcasing the raw intensity that defines NJPW's G1 Climax tournament.

This highly anticipated IWGP Heavyweight Championship match was another critical installment in the epic rivalry between Kazuchika Okada and Hiroshi Tanahashi. With the "Rainmaker" and the "Ace" constantly vying for supremacy, each encounter carried immense weight for the direction of New Japan Pro Wrestling. The match was a masterclass in storytelling and athleticism, further cementing their status as generational rivals.

This IWGP Heavyweight Championship match saw Hiroshi Tanahashi defend against Katsuyori Shibata, a deeply personal and highly anticipated encounter. Shibata, a former peer of Tanahashi's who had left NJPW, returned with a renewed, hard-hitting style, challenging the "Ace" for his throne. The match was a clash of philosophies and a battle for the soul of New Japan, with both men giving everything to prove their dominance.

This electrifying IWGP Intercontinental Championship match at Wrestle Kingdom 9 pitted the "King of Strong Style," Shinsuke Nakamura, against the captivating high-flyer Kota Ibushi. It was a dream match that saw Ibushi, a junior heavyweight sensation, challenge Nakamura's dominance in the heavyweight division. The clash of styles and the incredible athleticism on display created an unforgettable spectacle that stole the show.

This brutal NEVER Openweight Championship match was a testament to the fighting spirit of Tomoaki Honma and Tomohiro Ishii, two of New Japan's most resilient and hard-hitting competitors. Honma, a beloved underdog, was desperately seeking his first major singles championship, making the stakes incredibly high. The match was a relentless exchange of stiff strikes and headbutts, showcasing the raw passion and determination of both men.

The G1 Climax 25 Final saw a monumental clash between two of New Japan's biggest stars and former tag partners, Hiroshi Tanahashi and Shinsuke Nakamura. With the prestigious G1 trophy and a main event IWGP Heavyweight Championship shot at Wrestle Kingdom on the line, the stakes were astronomically high. This deeply personal and fiercely competitive encounter showcased their incredible history and cemented their legacies as top-tier rivals.

This Dream Gate Championship match at Dragon Gate's Gate of Destiny event pitted the seasoned veteran Masaaki Mochizuki against the powerful and dominant Shingo Takagi. It was a clash of generations and styles, with both men vying for the promotion's top prize. The bout was a hard-hitting affair, showcasing the unique athleticism and intensity that defines Dragon Gate's main event scene.

This main event at Wrestle Kingdom 10 was the epic culmination of the defining rivalry between Kazuchika Okada and Hiroshi Tanahashi, often hailed as the "passing of the torch" moment. With the IWGP Heavyweight Championship on the line, Okada sought to definitively establish his era, while Tanahashi fought to maintain his reign as the "Ace." It was a deeply emotional and historically significant encounter that shaped the future of New Japan Pro Wrestling.

This G1 Climax 26 encounter saw the reigning IWGP Heavyweight Champion Kazuchika Okada face the relentless and beloved underdog Tomohiro Ishii. Despite being a non-title match, the G1 points and bragging rights were immense, as Ishii consistently brought out the best in top stars. The match was a testament to Ishii's ability to challenge anyone, pushing Okada to his absolute limits in a hard-hitting, dramatic contest.

This G1 Climax 26 Final was a monumental clash between two of New Japan's fastest-rising stars, Kenny Omega and Tetsuya Naito, both vying for a main event title shot at Wrestle Kingdom. Omega, having recently formed The Elite, faced Naito, the leader of the wildly popular Los Ingobernables de Japon. The match was a breathtaking display of athleticism and storytelling, propelling both men to superstardom and setting the stage for future epic encounters.

match: Superkliq ( Adam Cole , Matt Jackson , and Nick Jackson ) vs. Matt Sydal , Ricochet , and Will Ospreay
description: This star-studded six-man tag team match at PWG's Battle of Los Angeles showcased an incredible array of top independent wrestling talent and high-flying athleticism. Featuring the Superkliq (Adam Cole and The Young Bucks) against the dynamic trio of Matt Sydal, Ricochet, and Will Ospreay, it was a non-stop, action-packed spectacle. The match perfectly encapsulated PWG's reputation for delivering exhilarating, must-see tag team contests.

Wrestle Kingdom 11 sets the stage for what would become a generation-defining rivalry. Kazuchika Okada stands at the absolute peak of the mountain as the Ace of New Japan Pro-Wrestling and the IWGP Heavyweight Champion. Stepping up to challenge his throne is Kenny Omega, the relentless leader of the Bullet Club, who just became the first foreign wrestler to win the grueling G1 Climax tournament. It is a clash of distinct ideologies and supreme athleticism, with the biggest prize in Japanese wrestling hanging in the balance.

This IWGP Intercontinental Championship match saw Tetsuya Naito, the leader of Los Ingobernables de Japon, defend his title against the powerful Canadian challenger, Michael Elgin. Naito's disdain for the title added a unique dynamic, while Elgin sought to prove his dominance in New Japan. The match was a hard-hitting and dramatic encounter, showcasing the contrasting styles of two top-tier competitors.

This IWGP Heavyweight Championship match was a brutal and emotionally charged encounter between Kazuchika Okada and Katsuyori Shibata, tragically becoming Shibata's final match. Shibata, known for his legitimate fighting style, pushed Okada to his absolute limits in a grueling contest that left a lasting impact. The match is remembered not only for its incredible intensity but also for the profound and unfortunate circumstances surrounding Shibata's career-ending injury.

event: Best of the Super Juniors Final
description: The Best of the Super Juniors Final pitted two of the world's premier junior heavyweights, Kushida and Will Ospreay, against each other in a breathtaking display of athleticism. The match was a high-flying, technical masterclass, showcasing the innovative and fast-paced style that defines New Japan's junior division. With the prestigious tournament trophy and a shot at the IWGP Junior Heavyweight Championship on the line, both men pushed themselves to the limit.

The second chapter of their legendary saga. Following their 46-minute epic at Wrestle Kingdom, Kenny Omega proved he belonged in the main event, but he failed to capture the title. Now, months later at Dominion, Omega finally gets his rematch. Both men have evolved; Okada brings new confidence as the undisputed kingpin, while Omega has studied his mistakes and is more desperate than ever to hit the elusive One-Winged Angel. The tension is palpable as they prepare to top the match that broke the rating scales.

This G1 Climax 27 Block Final was a high-stakes encounter between two of New Japan's biggest stars, Hiroshi Tanahashi and Tetsuya Naito, with the winner advancing to the tournament final. Naito, seeking to finally claim his spot as the undisputed top star, faced the resilient "Ace" Tanahashi in a dramatic clash. The match was a testament to their incredible chemistry and the intense competition to headline Wrestle Kingdom.

The third encounter in their incredible series takes place under completely different circumstances. In the G1 Climax 27 Block Final, Kazuchika Okada and Kenny Omega clash once more, but this time a strict 30-minute time limit looms overhead, and the IWGP Heavyweight Championship is not on the line. Instead, the winner secures a coveted spot in the G1 Final. With Omega desperate for a victory over Okada and the clock ticking away, this bout promises pure, unadulterated urgency from bell to bell.

This G1 Climax 27 Final was a highly anticipated clash between two of NJPW's biggest stars, Kenny Omega and Tetsuya Naito. Both men, having faced off in the previous year's G1 semi-final, sought to win the prestigious tournament and earn the right to challenge for the IWGP Heavyweight Championship at Wrestle Kingdom. It was a defining match that solidified their places at the top of the company.

This Battle of Los Angeles semi-final was a highly athletic big man rivalry that had captivated independent wrestling fans. Donovan Dijak and Keith Lee, known for their incredible chemistry and powerful, high-flying exchanges, battled for a spot in the prestigious BOLA final. Their encounter was a testament to their unique blend of power and agility.

This PWG encounter was a dream match between two of Europe's finest, Walter and Zack Sabre Jr., showcasing a stark clash of styles. Walter's brutal, hard-hitting offense met ZSJ's intricate, technical submission mastery in a high-stakes environment. It was a masterclass in contrasting wrestling philosophies.

The wrestling world was stunned when WWE legend Chris Jericho shockingly appeared in NJPW to challenge Kenny Omega in this 'Alpha vs. Omega' dream match. This groundbreaking inter-promotional spectacle brought unprecedented mainstream attention to Wrestle Kingdom. It was a pivotal moment that elevated Omega's global superstar status.

Johnny Gargano, still reeling from Tommaso Ciampa's betrayal, sought redemption and the NXT Championship against the dominant Andrade 'Cien' Almas. This emotionally charged, critically acclaimed match was a defining moment for Gargano, pushing him to his absolute physical and emotional limits. It solidified his reputation as 'Johnny Wrestling' in a desperate bid for glory.

match: Golden Lovers ( Kenny Omega and Kota Ibushi ) vs. The Young Bucks ( Matt Jackson and Nick Jackson )
description: The emotional reunion of the Golden Lovers, Kenny Omega and Kota Ibushi, created tension within The Elite, leading to this deeply personal tag team clash against The Young Bucks. This match was a battle of brotherhood and loyalty, as four best friends were torn between their tag partners and the future of their faction. The outcome had significant implications for all involved.

This IWGP Junior Heavyweight Championship match was another intense chapter in the long-standing rivalry between Will Ospreay and Marty Scurll. Dubbed the 'Aerial Assassin' vs. 'Villain' feud, their contrasting styles and deep personal history fueled this high-stakes encounter. Both men aimed to assert their dominance in the junior heavyweight division.

match: Adam Cole vs. EC3 vs. Killian Dain vs. Lars Sullivan vs. Ricochet vs. Velveteen Dream
description: The inaugural NXT North American Championship was on the line in this groundbreaking ladder match featuring six of NXT's most promising stars. Adam Cole, EC3, Killian Dain, Lars Sullivan, Ricochet, and Velveteen Dream battled in a chaotic, high-stakes encounter. The match was designed to crown a new champion and establish a new tier of prestige within the brand.

A blood feud built on the ultimate betrayal. Once beloved tag team partners known as DIY, Tommaso Ciampa shocked the world by turning on Johnny Gargano in the most vicious way imaginable. For nearly a year, Ciampa tormented Gargano, costing him matches and his NXT career. Now, in an unsanctioned, unsanctioned, anything-goes match at NXT TakeOver: New Orleans, Gargano finally gets his hands on his former best friend. There are no rules, only pure hatred, as they look to settle the score once and for all.

This match marked a significant moment for Spanish wrestling, as promising young talent A-Kid faced one of the world's premier technical wrestlers, Zack Sabre Jr., in his home promotion. It was a huge test for A-Kid, showcasing his skill against a globally recognized master of submissions. The encounter put Spanish wrestling on a larger stage.

Another iconic chapter in the legendary rivalry between 'The Ace' Hiroshi Tanahashi and 'The Rainmaker' Kazuchika Okada, two pillars of NJPW. Okada defended his record-breaking IWGP Heavyweight Championship reign against his greatest rival, adding another layer to their historic feud. The match further cemented their status as generational rivals.

event: Best of the Super Juniors Final
description: The highly anticipated final of the Best of the Super Juniors tournament pitted Taiji Ishimori against the dynamic Hiromu Takahashi. The winner would earn a coveted shot at the IWGP Junior Heavyweight Championship, making this a high-stakes, fast-paced encounter. Both men pushed their limits in a bid for divisional supremacy.

The ultimate climax to arguably the greatest wrestling rivalry in history. Okada and Omega meet one final time to settle the score at Dominion, with the series tied up. To ensure a definitive winner and leave no doubt about who is truly the best wrestler in the world, they agree to a monumental stipulation: a 2-out-of-3 Falls match with absolutely no time limit. The stakes, the history, and the sheer physical toll leading into this match make it the most anticipated bout of the decade.

match: Moustache Mountain ( Trent Seven and Tyler Bate ) vs. The Undisputed Era ( Kyle O'Reilly and Roderick Strong )
description: This highly anticipated NXT Tag Team Championship match showcased two of the best tag teams in WWE at the time: Moustache Mountain and The Undisputed Era. It was a brutal, hard-hitting affair that elevated both teams and the prestige of the NXT tag titles. The match was a testament to the strength of NXT's tag team division.

This G1 Climax 28 block match was a highly anticipated rematch from the G1 Climax 27 Final between Kenny Omega and Tetsuya Naito. Both NJPW superstars were looking to gain crucial points early in the tournament and establish dominance. It was a pivotal encounter in their quest to win the prestigious G1 Climax.

In this G1 Climax block match, Hirooki Goto, a perennial contender, sought to prove himself against the reigning IWGP Heavyweight Champion, Kenny Omega. It was a crucial opportunity for Goto to score a significant upset and climb the ranks in the competitive tournament. The match showcased Goto's fighting spirit against the champion.

This G1 Climax block match was a hard-hitting, intensely physical encounter between two of NJPW's toughest heavyweights, Hirooki Goto and Tomohiro Ishii. Known for their strong style and relentless offense, it was a guaranteed brutal affair for crucial tournament points. The match delivered on its promise of a visceral battle.

This G1 Climax block match pitted the reigning IWGP Heavyweight Champion, Kenny Omega, against the resilient 'Stone Pitbull' Tomohiro Ishii. Ishii, known for his striking and never-say-die attitude, aimed to prove he could defeat the top champion. It was a brutal test of endurance and will for Omega.

Another iconic clash in their legendary rivalry, this G1 Climax block final between Hiroshi Tanahashi and Kazuchika Okada determined who would advance to the tournament finals. The stakes were incredibly high, as both men aimed to secure their spot and potentially challenge for the IWGP Heavyweight Championship. It was a monumental encounter in their storied feud.

The emotional and highly anticipated 'Golden Lovers' clash in the G1 Climax block final saw Kenny Omega battle Kota Ibushi, with the winner advancing to the tournament final. This deeply personal match tested the bonds of their friendship as best friends and tag partners battled for supremacy. It was a heartbreaking yet exhilarating display of competition.

The grand final of the G1 Climax 28 tournament pitted the veteran 'Ace' Hiroshi Tanahashi against the dynamic 'Golden Star' Kota Ibushi. The winner would earn the coveted Tokyo Dome IWGP Heavyweight Championship challenge, making this a career-defining match for both men. It was a clash of generations for ultimate glory.

Following Tanahashi's G1 Climax victory, he defended his Tokyo Dome IWGP Heavyweight Championship challenge contract against his greatest rival, Kazuchika Okada. This match was a crucial test for Tanahashi, as Okada sought to reclaim his spot at the top and deny 'The Ace' his main event opportunity. The stakes were incredibly high for both men.

match: Chaos ( Kazuchika Okada and Tomohiro Ishii ) vs. Golden Lovers ( Kenny Omega and Kota Ibushi )
description: This dream tag team match featured four of NJPW's top stars, representing their respective factions and rivalries: Kazuchika Okada and Tomohiro Ishii of CHAOS against the Golden Lovers, Kenny Omega and Kota Ibushi. It was a high-profile encounter showcasing incredible talent and setting the stage for future singles clashes. The match delivered on its promise of star power.

match: Hiroshi Tanahashi and Will Ospreay vs. Golden Lovers ( Kenny Omega and Kota Ibushi )
description: This star-studded tag team preview match led up to Wrestle Kingdom 13, featuring IWGP Heavyweight Champion Kenny Omega and his challenger Hiroshi Tanahashi on opposing sides. With Kota Ibushi and Will Ospreay adding another layer of excitement, the match built intense anticipation for their main event clash. It was a taste of the epic showdown to come.

The main event of Wrestle Kingdom 13 saw Kenny Omega defend the IWGP Heavyweight Championship against the G1 Climax winner, Hiroshi Tanahashi. This was a clash of eras and ideologies, with Omega representing the 'new era' and Tanahashi the traditional 'Ace' of NJPW. It was a battle for the soul of the company.

The final of the prestigious New Japan Cup saw Kazuchika Okada face Sanada, with the winner earning a shot at the IWGP Heavyweight Championship. This match was a significant opportunity for Sanada to finally overcome his long-time rival, Okada, and step into the main event picture. It was a pivotal moment in Sanada's career.

The vacant NXT Championship was on the line in this critically acclaimed 2-out-of-3 Falls match between Adam Cole and Johnny Gargano. Following Tommaso Ciampa's injury, Gargano sought to finally capture the title, while Cole aimed to solidify The Undisputed Era's dominance. It was a defining moment for both competitors in a grueling contest.

event: Best of the Super Juniors Night 8
description: This Best of the Super Juniors block match was a highly anticipated encounter between two of the most athletic and innovative high-flyers in professional wrestling, Will Ospreay and Bandido. The match was a breathtaking showcase of aerial offense and incredible speed, crucial for tournament points. It lived up to its billing as a high-flying spectacle.

A fiercely emotional blood feud between brothers fighting for their family's legacy. Cody Rhodes, having departed WWE to help build AEW, looks to symbolically kill the 'Attitude Era' by defeating his older brother Dustin. For Dustin, this is a deeply personal fight to prove he is more than just his past bizarre personas and that he can still go in the ring with the very best. With decades of complicated family history and contrasting career paths bubbling to the surface, the emotional weight inside the arena is staggering before the bell even rings.

This NXT Championship rematch saw Adam Cole challenge Johnny Gargano following their epic encounter at TakeOver: New York. Cole, still seeking his first NXT Championship, faced Gargano in another high-stakes, critically acclaimed main event. It was a continuation of their intense rivalry for NXT's top prize.

event: Best of the Super Juniors Final
description: The highly anticipated final of the Best of the Super Juniors tournament pitted the undefeated powerhouse Shingo Takagi against the reigning IWGP Junior Heavyweight Champion, Will Ospreay. This was a clash of titans, with both men pushing their limits to win the prestigious tournament. It was a defining match for the junior heavyweight division.

This highly anticipated IWGP Junior Heavyweight Championship match featured two of the world's most dynamic junior heavyweights, Dragon Lee and Will Ospreay. It was a fast-paced, high-flying spectacle, with both men looking to assert their dominance in the division. The match delivered an incredible display of athleticism.

This G1 Climax block match was a dream encounter between two of NJPW's most exciting and athletic stars, Kota Ibushi and Will Ospreay. Both known for their incredible high-flying and hard-hitting styles, this was a crucial encounter for tournament points and a potential show-stealer. It showcased the best of modern wrestling.

This brutal, hard-hitting G1 Climax block match pitted Jon Moxley, making his G1 debut, against the 'Stone Pitbull' Tomohiro Ishii. It was a test of Moxley's mettle against one of NJPW's toughest, showcasing a raw, violent style. The match was a pure display of fighting spirit and strong style.

This highly anticipated G1 Climax block match featured the reigning IWGP Heavyweight Champion, Kazuchika Okada, against his former Chaos stablemate, Will Ospreay. It was a significant test for Ospreay, proving he could compete with the top heavyweights, and a crucial match for Okada's tournament run. The match highlighted Ospreay's ascent to the main event.

match: Bandido , Flamita , and Rey Horus vs. Black Taurus , Laredo Kid , and Puma King
description: This high-octane six-man tag team match featured some of the best luchadores and high-flyers on the independent scene: Bandido, Flamita, and Rey Horus against Black Taurus, Laredo Kid, and Puma King. It was a chaotic, breathtaking display of athleticism and teamwork, a signature PWG spectacle. The match was a non-stop thrill ride.

Another chapter in their ongoing rivalry, this G1 Climax block match saw Sanada desperately trying to finally defeat Kazuchika Okada. A victory here would be a monumental step for Sanada in his quest to reach the G1 final and challenge for the IWGP Heavyweight Championship. The match was crucial for Sanada's main event aspirations.

This eagerly awaited LIJ vs. LIJ clash in the G1 Climax pitted the leader Tetsuya Naito against the powerhouse Shingo Takagi. The match was a test of loyalty and dominance within the faction, with both men needing crucial points in the tournament. It was a compelling internal struggle for supremacy.

This brutal, hard-hitting G1 Climax block match featured two of NJPW's most intense and physically dominant competitors: Shingo Takagi and Tomohiro Ishii. It was a pure strong style slugfest, with both men refusing to back down in their pursuit of tournament points. The match was a testament to their incredible toughness.

This highly anticipated G1 Climax block final pitted the reigning IWGP Heavyweight Champion, Kazuchika Okada, against the charismatic Kota Ibushi. The winner would advance to the G1 Climax final, making this a monumental, high-stakes encounter between two of NJPW's biggest stars. It was a defining moment in the tournament.

Kota Ibushi, the 'Golden Star,' faced the cunning 'Switchblade' Jay White in the G1 Climax 29 Final, with the coveted Wrestle Kingdom main event contract on the line. White had consistently undermined NJPW's top stars, while Ibushi sought to prove his destiny as a main event player. This match was a clash of ideologies, determining who would challenge for the IWGP Heavyweight Championship.

WALTER, the dominant NXT UK Champion, defended his title against the resilient Tyler Bate in a highly anticipated clash of styles. Bate, a former champion and the heart of NXT UK, aimed to reclaim the gold from the imposing leader of Imperium. This match was a brutal test of endurance and will, showcasing the best of the brand.

match: Lucha Brothers ( Pentagón Jr. and Rey Fenix ) vs. The Young Bucks ( Matt Jackson and Nick Jackson )
description: The high-flying Lucha Brothers collided with the innovative Young Bucks in a brutal Ladder Match for the AAA World Tag Team Championship. Their rivalry had spanned promotions, marked by incredible athleticism and a fierce desire to prove who was the best tag team in the world. This encounter at AEW's inaugural All Out was a chaotic, career-defining spectacle.

Two of the world's most electrifying luchadores, Bandido and Dragon Lee, met in the highly anticipated final of PWG's Battle of Los Angeles tournament. Both men had battled through a grueling field to reach this point, with the prestigious BOLA trophy and a future PWG World Title shot on the line. This match was a showcase of breathtaking athleticism and non-stop action.

David Starr, the self-proclaimed 'Independent Wrestling's Last Real Man,' faced Irish wrestling's prodigy, Jordan Devlin, in a highly charged main event for OTT's 5th Year Anniversary. Starr's anti-establishment rhetoric clashed with Devlin's homegrown hero status, creating a deeply personal and intense rivalry. This match was a testament to the passion and quality of the European independent scene.

Will Ospreay defended his IWGP Junior Heavyweight Championship against the returning Hiromu Takahashi, who made his emotional comeback from a career-threatening neck injury. Hiromu immediately set his sights on Ospreay, seeking to reclaim the title he never lost. This highly anticipated clash at Wrestle Kingdom 14 was a testament to both men's resilience and incredible skill.

Kazuchika Okada, the reigning IWGP Heavyweight Champion, faced Kota Ibushi, the G1 Climax 29 winner, in a monumental main event on Night 1 of Wrestle Kingdom 14. Ibushi aimed to finally capture NJPW's top prize and solidify his place as a main event star. The winner would advance to challenge for both the Heavyweight and Intercontinental titles on Night 2, adding immense stakes to this dream match.

Tetsuya Naito finally achieved his destiny, challenging Kazuchika Okada in the main event of Wrestle Kingdom 14 Night 2 for both the IWGP Heavyweight and Intercontinental Championships. Naito had spent years fighting for this moment, seeking to main event the Tokyo Dome and capture the title that had eluded him. This was the emotional climax of the 'Double Gold Dash,' a true redemption story for El Ingobernable.

match: Will Ospreay vs. Zack Sabre Jr.
description: The aerial assassin Will Ospreay clashed with the technical wizard Zack Sabre Jr. for the RevPro British Heavyweight Championship. Their contrasting styles and long-standing rivalry always guaranteed a captivating encounter, with Sabre's submission prowess battling Ospreay's breathtaking athleticism. This 'High Stakes' match was a testament to their incredible chemistry and mastery of their respective crafts.

match: Kenny Omega and "Hangman" Adam Page vs. The Young Bucks ( Matt Jackson and Nick Jackson )
description: One of the most complex, emotionally layered tag team rivalries in history. The Elite, once an unbreakable brotherhood, finds itself fractured. 'Hangman' Adam Page is spiraling, plagued by self-doubt and feeling alienated from his friends, finding solace only in alcohol and an uneasy alliance with Kenny Omega. On the other side are The Young Bucks, the foundation of AEW, who feel Page has abandoned them. This match isn't just for the AEW World Tag Team Championship; it is a heartbreaking collision of friends whose insecurities and resentments have finally boiled over.

match: Best Friends ( Chuck Taylor and Trent ) vs. Santana and Ortiz
description: Best Friends (Chuck Taylor and Trent) faced Santana and Ortiz in a brutal Street Fight, aiming to settle their intense rivalry that had spilled over for weeks. With no rules to hold them back, both teams unleashed a torrent of violence and creativity. This match was a chaotic, hardcore spectacle designed to bring their bitter feud to a decisive end.

Shingo Takagi and Will Ospreay, fresh off their legendary Best of the Super Juniors final, rekindled their rivalry in a highly anticipated G1 Climax 30 encounter. Both men had since transitioned to the heavyweight division, promising an even more explosive and hard-hitting affair. This tournament match was a crucial test of their heavyweight prowess and a continuation of their incredible chemistry.

Kota Ibushi faced the terrifying Minoru Suzuki in a brutal G1 Climax 30 encounter that pushed both men to their physical limits. Suzuki's sadistic strikes and submissions clashed with Ibushi's 'Golden Star' resilience and willingness to absorb punishment. This match was a visceral display of fighting spirit, a true test of who could withstand the most pain.

The 'Rainmaker' Kazuchika Okada clashed with 'The Dragon' Shingo Takagi in a highly anticipated first-time singles encounter during the G1 Climax 30. Both men, at the peak of their powers, sought to secure crucial points in the prestigious tournament. This match was a monumental clash of NJPW's elite, showcasing their incredible strength and skill.

WALTER, the dominant NXT UK Champion, faced the unhinged intensity of Ilja Dragunov in a brutal, hard-hitting encounter that immediately captivated audiences. Dragunov's relentless offense and willingness to absorb punishment made him a unique threat to WALTER's reign. This match was a visceral display of European strong style, laying the groundwork for one of WWE's most compelling rivalries.

match: The Young Bucks ( Matt Jackson and Nick Jackson ) vs. FTR ( Cash Wheeler and Dax Harwood )
description: The Young Bucks finally faced FTR in a long-awaited dream match for the AEW World Tag Team Championship, a clash of tag team philosophies. FTR, the self-proclaimed 'best tag team,' sought to prove their traditional, no-nonsense style was superior to the Bucks' high-flying, innovative approach. This was a battle for tag team supremacy, years in the making, with the titles and bragging rights on the line.

Go Shiozaki defended his GHC Heavyweight Championship against the veteran legend Takashi Sugiura in a grueling main event for Pro Wrestling Noah. Shiozaki's dominant reign was challenged by Sugiura's relentless, hard-hitting style, creating a clash of generations and fighting spirit. This match was a testament to Noah's strong style tradition and the resilience of both champions.

Kazuchika Okada faced his former protégé Will Ospreay in a deeply personal grudge match at Wrestle Kingdom 15 Night 1. Ospreay had shockingly betrayed Okada, forming the United Empire and declaring himself the future of NJPW. This was Okada's chance for revenge and to reassert his dominance over the man who dared to challenge his legacy.

Tetsuya Naito defended both the IWGP Heavyweight and Intercontinental Championships against Kota Ibushi, the G1 Climax 30 winner, in a high-stakes main event at Wrestle Kingdom 15 Night 1. Ibushi sought to achieve his 'God' status by capturing both titles, while Naito aimed to continue his reign as the double champion. This match was a thrilling clash of NJPW's top stars, with the ultimate prize on the line.

Shingo Takagi defended his NEVER Openweight Championship against the powerhouse Jeff Cobb in a brutal, hard-hitting encounter at Wrestle Kingdom 15 Night 2. Takagi's relentless offense met Cobb's incredible strength and athleticism, promising a collision of two immovable forces. This match was a pure display of heavyweight strong style, with both men leaving everything in the ring.

Kota Ibushi, having defeated Tetsuya Naito on Night 1, defended both the IWGP Heavyweight and Intercontinental Championships against 'Switchblade' Jay White, who had overcome Kazuchika Okada. White had consistently tormented Ibushi, and this was their final confrontation for the undisputed top prize in NJPW. This match was the culmination of a long-standing rivalry, with Ibushi's dream of being 'God' hanging in the balance.

Kenny Omega, the newly crowned AEW World Champion and 'Belt Collector,' defended his title against the electrifying Rey Fenix in a highly anticipated main event. Fenix, one half of the Lucha Brothers, brought his unparalleled athleticism and daredevil style to challenge Omega's calculated dominance. This match was a spectacular clash of styles, kicking off the new year with a bang.
